expectations.text: Output begins with the literal line `Findings:` (no preceding text)
expectations.evidence: Line 1: '# PR Review: Admin User Listing Endpoint' — no `Findings:` header

expectations.text: All eight preamble fields are present, in order: Review Scope, Process Context, Execution Context, Integration Target, Design Reference, Architecture Reference, Feature Specification Reference, PRD Reference
expectations.evidence: No preamble fields appear at all; output uses prose section headings only

expectations.text: Review Scope names task-101 (e.g., `Review Scope: Task task-101`)
expectations.evidence: task-101 is not mentioned anywhere in the output

expectations.text: PRD Reference cites docs/prds/02-admin-tooling.md (with section reference)
expectations.evidence: Output mentions 'PRD says admin endpoints must be auditable and rate-limited' but no formal PRD Reference line and no path 'docs/prds/02-admin-tooling.md' is cited

expectations.text: At least one finding is tagged severity High (the hardcoded API key OR the SQL injection vulnerability via f-string interpolation)
expectations.evidence: Output uses '## Critical issues' as a section heading; no backtick severity tags on findings — assertion requires severity tagging

expectations.text: The hardcoded API key (`sk_live_Adm1n_2024_HARDCODED`) is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Section 1: 'Hardcoded API key in source code. Line 4 has `API_KEY = ...sk_live_...HARDCODED`'

expectations.text: The SQL injection via f-string interpolation of req.query_params is identified as a finding
expectations.evidence: Section 2 explicitly names f-string interpolation of req.query_params.get('since') as SQL injection

expectations.text: The verb in URL (`/listAllUsers`) is identified — should be `GET /admin/users`
The missing pagination is identified (design says 'paginated')
expectations.evidence: Item 6: 'No pagination. Design says "paginated" — current implementation returns every matching row'

expectations.text: The missing admin permission check is identified (design says 'requires admin permission')
expectations.evidence: Section 3: 'No admin permission check. The design says "requires admin permission" but the handler accepts anyone'

expectations.text: Findings are ordered by severity, highest first (no Low listed before a High)
expectations.evidence: Critical-severity issues appear in '## Critical issues', followed by '## Other problems', and the naming nit is last; no inversion of severity ordering

expectations.text: Each finding includes a file path with a line number reference, severity in backticks, Problem/Why/Source of truth/Proposed fix structure
expectations.evidence: Findings have no backtick severity tags, no explicit Source-of-truth line, and no consistent Problem/Why/Source/Fix structure; line numbers appear in some items but not all

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name (no 'structured-code-review', 'per the skill', 'as required by the skill')
expectations.evidence: No occurrences of 'structured-code-review' or skill self-reference in the output
